payload.description: Initialized the local Violence Checker Git repository and created the minimum runnable Python Streamlit scaffold for the Phase 0 Semantic Violence Detection Pre-PoC without implementing analysis logic or external API calls.

operations.action: Initialized a local Git repository in the repository root.
operations.reason: The brief required the local Violence Checker directory to be a Git work tree.

operations.action: Created minimal source and telemetry directories.
operations.reason: The scaffold required a Python application module location and a telemetry heartbeat file path.

operations.action: Created app.py with a minimal Streamlit entry point.
operations.reason: The brief required a Streamlit interface that identifies the Phase 0 Pre-PoC, states it is an initialization scaffold, and performs no incident analysis or OpenAI calls.

operations.action: Created src/config.py with dotenv-based root .env loading and a Pydantic configuration model.
operations.reason: The brief required centralized typed configuration loading that does not require OPENAI_API_KEY during this phase and does not expose environment values.

operations.action: Used typing.Optional annotations in src/config.py.
operations.reason: Local verification showed the active Python interpreter did not support the newer union annotation syntax, so Optional preserved the required typed configuration while allowing compilation and import checks to pass.

operations.action: Created .env.example with OPENAI_API_KEY and OPENAI_MODEL placeholders only.
operations.reason: The brief required placeholder configuration names without real or sample credentials.

operations.action: Created .gitignore excluding .env, Python bytecode, cache directories, virtual environments, Streamlit local secrets, and common operating-system metadata.
operations.reason: The brief required secrets and local runtime artifacts to stay out of Git.

operations.action: Created requirements.txt containing streamlit, openai, pydantic, and python-dotenv only.
operations.reason: The brief required a dependency manifest limited to the approved stack and current scaffold.

operations.action: Created README.md with purpose, current status, non-production notice, setup instructions, startup command, repository structure, and intentionally unimplemented capabilities.
operations.reason: The brief required clear local setup and startup documentation for the initialization scaffold.

operations.action: Created telemetry/executor_heartbeat.jsonl and appended this single JSONL event after implementation and verification.
operations.reason: The brief required exactly one self-contained executor heartbeat event recording repository mutations.

payload.description: Completed the next bounded OPORD 001 execution cycle by establishing a repository-local Python runtime, adding canonical incident and violence finding contracts, adding the approved synthetic fixture library, implementing an illustrative lexical regex baseline, adding automated tests, verifying the Streamlit scaffold in the local environment, and preserving the project as a local pre-PoC demonstration.

operations.action: Created a repository-local virtual environment at .venv.
operations.reason: The brief required an isolated local Python environment inside the repository.

operations.action: Installed streamlit, openai, pydantic, python-dotenv, and pytest into .venv.
operations.reason: The brief required the approved runtime dependencies and pytest to be available in the repository-local environment.

operations.action: Updated requirements.txt to include pytest.
operations.reason: The brief allowed pytest as a test dependency and required the dependency manifest to declare dependencies needed to run the application and tests.

operations.action: Created src/models.py with Incident, ViolenceFinding, event type enum, and intentionality enum.
operations.reason: The brief required canonical Pydantic contracts for incident input and later structured violence finding output with bounded values and validation rules.

operations.action: Created src/fixtures.py containing exactly eight approved synthetic incidents and qualitative metadata.
operations.reason: The brief required a simple importable fixture library with stable identifiers and exact authorized narratives.

operations.action: Created src/regex_baseline.py with a deterministic case-insensitive lexical baseline.
operations.reason: The brief required a reusable illustrative regex baseline returning detected, matched_terms, and matched_patterns while avoiding semantic resolution.

operations.action: Created tests/test_config_and_app.py, tests/test_models.py, tests/test_fixtures.py, and tests/test_regex_baseline.py.
operations.reason: The brief required automated coverage for configuration, app import behavior, schemas, fixtures, and regex baseline behavior.

operations.action: Updated README.md with virtual environment setup, dependency installation, test command, fixture description, domain model description, regex baseline description, regex limitations, and the absence of semantic extraction.
operations.reason: The brief required documentation for the new local runtime and implemented pre-PoC surfaces.

operations.action: Ran pytest, which created .pytest_cache inside the repository.
operations.reason: The brief required running the automated test suite, and pytest created its standard local cache as an automatic verification artifact.

operations.action: Appended this single executor heartbeat event to telemetry/executor_heartbeat.jsonl after implementation and verification.
operations.reason: The brief required exactly one additional self-contained JSONL event recording repository mutations from this execution.

payload.description: Implemented the isolated semantic violence extraction pipeline for the Phase 0 pre-PoC, including centralized model configuration, centralized prompt instructions, typed extraction results, OpenAI Responses API structured-output integration, deterministic offline tests, optional live smoke-test command, documentation updates, and verification without executing a live provider request because no repository-local environment file was present.

operations.action: Updated src/config.py to define a documented default OpenAI model and keep OPENAI_API_KEY optional until semantic extraction is invoked.
operations.reason: The brief required centralized configuration for OPENAI_API_KEY and OPENAI_MODEL, with credentials optional during import and offline tests.

operations.action: Created src/semantic_prompt.py with centralized semantic extraction instructions.
operations.reason: The brief required a dedicated prompt that analyzes only the supplied narrative, distinguishes event types and context, identifies negation and corrections, preserves exact evidence excerpts, and produces ViolenceFinding-compatible output.

operations.action: Created src/semantic_extractor.py with extract_violence_finding and typed SemanticExtractionResult statuses.
operations.reason: The brief required an isolated semantic extractor that calls the OpenAI Responses API once per incident, requests structured ViolenceFinding output, validates through Pydantic, and returns explicit success or failure results.

operations.action: Created scripts/__init__.py and scripts/live_smoke_test.py.
operations.reason: The brief required an explicitly invoked optional live smoke-test command using CASE_008 that does not run during pytest and prints only non-secret status and result data.

operations.action: Created tests/test_semantic_extractor.py with deterministic mocked provider coverage.
operations.reason: The brief required offline tests for configuration behavior, request construction, successful structured output, failure categories, provider-call count, unchanged narrative input, metadata exclusion, and isolation from Streamlit and regex logic.

operations.action: Updated README.md with semantic extraction architecture, .env configuration, default model behavior, offline test behavior, live smoke-test command, Pydantic validation guarantees, malformed-output handling, and current exclusions.
operations.reason: The brief required documentation of the semantic extraction pipeline and its limitations without adding UI comparison or Salesforce preview work.

operations.action: Classified Pydantic validation errors raised during SDK parsing as validation failures.
operations.reason: The extractor needed to preserve the brief's distinction between provider request failures and malformed structured output that fails Pydantic validation.

operations.action: Replaced literal test credential placeholders with an indirect constant.
operations.reason: The repository secret scan needed to remain clean while preserving deterministic tests for credential-present control flow.

operations.action: Ran pytest, which updated .pytest_cache inside the repository.
operations.reason: The brief required the full automated test suite to run, and pytest updates its standard local cache during verification.

operations.action: Appended this single executor heartbeat event to telemetry/executor_heartbeat.jsonl after implementation and verification.
operations.reason: The brief required exactly one additional self-contained JSONL event recording repository mutations from this execution.

operations.action: Verified .env exists, is ignored by Git, does not appear in git status, and contains no credential value without printing file contents.

payload.description: Performed one bounded live verification of the semantic violence extraction pipeline using the repository-local configuration and CASE_008, confirmed a successful validated ViolenceFinding result from a single existing smoke-test invocation, ran the automated tests, and recorded the verification outcome without modifying application files or exposing credentials.

operations.action: Inspected repository status before live verification.
operations.reason: The brief required checking the repository state before executing the bounded live smoke test.

operations.action: Confirmed .env exists and configuration resolves a non-empty API credential without printing the credential value.
operations.reason: The brief required credential readiness verification while forbidding exposure of the secret value.

operations.action: Confirmed the configured model name was gpt-5-mini.
operations.reason: The brief required reporting the model name while keeping credentials secret.

operations.action: Executed .venv/bin/python -m scripts.live_smoke_test exactly once.
operations.reason: The brief required one live OpenAI Responses API verification using only the approved CASE_008 narrative through the existing smoke-test command.

operations.action: Observed a success result containing a validated ViolenceFinding with a valid attempted physical violence event type, confidence within bounds, and evidence excerpts present.
operations.reason: The brief required confirming the live result category and validation properties when the smoke test succeeded.

operations.action: Confirmed by source inspection that the smoke command selects CASE_008, does not submit fixture metadata, and invokes the extractor once, and that the extractor issues one Responses API parse request per invocation using the unmodified incident narrative.
operations.reason: The brief required confirming only CASE_008 was submitted, metadata was not submitted, the original narrative was preserved, and no more than one provider request occurred.

operations.action: Ran .venv/bin/python -m pytest after the live call; the command completed with one configuration test failure caused by the local .env credential being loaded during that test.
operations.reason: The brief required running the existing automated test suite after the live call and preserving the non-secret result without modifying tests or application code.

operations.action: Ran PYTHON_DOTENV_DISABLED=1 .venv/bin/python -m pytest, which passed all tests.
operations.reason: The brief required confirming no repository regression, and this run disabled local .env loading without changing repository files.

operations.action: Verified no apparent credential exists in repository files outside ignored .env and inspected final git status.
